name: Taylor expansion of exact solution equals numerical method
subsection_title: The Taylor expansion of the exact solution
statement_text:
The Taylor expansion of yn given by (311b) and (311c) is
equal to
                          
                          n                       1
        yn = y(x0 ) +           hi ξ i                   F (t)(y(x0 )) + O(hn+1 ).   (311d)
                          i=1
                                                σ(t)γ(t)
                                         t∈Ti
proof_text:
Proof. The case n = 0 is obvious. We now use induction and suppose that
(311d) is true with n replaced by n − 1. By Lemma 310B, with
                                                          1
                                                θ(t) =        ,
                                                         γ(t)
we have as the coefficient of F (t)(y(x0 ))hr(t) , the expression
          ξ
                 1                              1                    1 r(t)
             %k            ξ r(t)−1 dξ =      %k           ξ r(t) =      ξ ,
          0    i=1 γ(t i )               r(t)  i=1 γ(t i )          γ(t)

where t = [t1 t2 · · · tk ].
proof_latex:
\begin{proof}
The case $n = 0$ is obvious. We now use induction and suppose that \eqref{eq:311d} is true with $n$ replaced by $n - 1$. By Lemma~\ref{lem:310B}, with
\[
\theta(t) = \frac{1}{\gamma(t)},
\]
we have as the coefficient of $F(t)(y(x_0))h^{r(t)}$, the expression
\[
\int_0^\xi \prod_{i=1}^k \frac{1}{\gamma(t_i)} \xi^{r(t)-1} d\xi = \frac{1}{r(t)} \prod_{i=1}^k \frac{1}{\gamma(t_i)} \xi^{r(t)} = \frac{1}{\gamma(t)} \xi^{r(t)},
\]
where $t = [t_1 t_2 \cdots t_k]$.
\end{proof}

variables.name: y
variables.definition: solution of the differential equation, y(x) or y(x0 + hξ)

variables.name: y_n
variables.definition: n-th Picard iterate defined by (311b) and (311c)

variables.name: ξ
variables.definition: variable in [0,1] for the Picard integral equation

variables.name: f
variables.definition: vector field f(y) defining the ODE y' = f(y)

variables.name: F(t)
variables.definition: elementary differential associated with tree t

variables.name: σ(t)
variables.definition: symmetry of tree t (number of equivalent labellings)

variables.name: T_i
variables.definition: set of unlabelled trees of order i

equations.content: y_n(x0 + hξ) = y(x0) + h ∫_0^ξ f(y_{n-1}(x0 + hξ)) dξ

preamble: The theorem gives the Taylor expansion of the n-th Picard iterate y_n for solving y' = f(y) via the Picard integral equation, using elementary differentials F(t) over trees t of order i, with combinatorial factors σ(t) and γ(t).
